description_long: This object is a cylindrical stone barrel or bucket with a flat, slightly uneven top surface and vertical sides. The exterior is adorned with intricate, shallowly carved hieroglyphic-like symbols and figures, giving it an ancient or archaeological appearance. The carvings are more pronounced and detailed on one side, while the other sides show more worn or faded engravings. The surface texture is rough and matte, with visible weathering and some small chips or imperfections. The color is a sandy beige with subtle variations, resembling natural stone. The object appears solid and heavy, likely carved from a single block of stone, and lacks any visible handles or lid.
description: A cylindrical stone barrel or bucket with carved hieroglyphic symbols, rough matte surface, and a sandy beige color.
description_view_0: Front view shows the most detailed and prominent hieroglyphic carvings on the surface.
description_view_1: This side displays more faded carvings and a smoother, less detailed surface.
description_view_2: Carvings are visible but less pronounced, with a central seam or worn area.
description_view_3: This view shows faint carvings and a small chip or indentation on the side.
